items.title: Ringgit firmer against US dollar as stronger export performance lifts market sentiment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit firmed against the US dollar as market sentiment improved on the back of Malaysia's trade performance, which rose 28.6 per cent year-on-year to a record RM336.73 billion in April 2026. Malaysia's trade maintained strong momentum in April 2026 with exports and imports reaching record highs despite heightened global uncertainties, including geopolitical tensions in Middle East that led to higher logistics costs, supply chain disruptions and commodity price volatility. MITI also said exports extended their growth momentum for the 10th consecutive month, surging 36.9 per cent to a record RM182.74 billion, surpassing the previous high of RM152.77 billion recorded in December 2025 by RM30 billion, while imports rose 20 per cent to RM153.99 billion.

items.title: King bestows Johor honours on Che' Puan Mahkota Khaleeda, Raja Muda
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today conferred the "Darjah Sultan Ibrahim Johor Yang Amat Disanjungi Pangkat Pertama Dato' Sri Mulia Sultan Ibrahim Johor (SMIJ)" state title upon Che' Puan Mahkota Khaleeda Johor, wife of Johor Regent Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim. According to a post on the official Facebook page of HRH Crown Prince of Johor, His Majesty also conferred the "Darjah Kerabat Johor Yang Amat Dihormati Pangkat Pertama (DK I)" upon the Raja Muda of Johor, Tunku Iskandar Tunku Ismail. Also in attendance were Tunku Temenggong of Johor, Tunku Idris Iskandar Al-Haj Sultan Ibrahim, and Tunku Abu Bakar Ibrahim Tunku Ismail.

items.title: Gardenia to relocate bakery production from Singapore to Johor Baru
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Gardenia Foods (S) Pte Ltd on Wednesday announced that it will shift its bakery production from Singapore to Johor Baru, as its Pandan Loop manufacturing facility will cease operations on June 30. The company said the move would result in the retrenchment of 141 employees at the Pandan Loop facility. It added that Singapore will remain Gardenia's central hub for key functions.

items.title: Federal Court affirms 30-year sentence for ex-Rela member who shot dead friend at Terengganu temple
items.summary: A three-judge panel chaired by Datuk Rhodzariah Bujang unanimously dismissed the appeal of Ong Chow Lee, 62, to set aside the murder conviction decided by the Court of Appeal on July 2, 2025. “The court finds that the appellant’s appeal has no merit. The Court of Appeal on July 2, 2025 dismissed the appellant’s appeal against the conviction and sentence and confirmed the previous decision of the High Court.

items.title: Dumping of domestic waste blamed for pollution in Penang's Sungai Rambai
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: Dumping of domestic waste into drains and rivers is believed to be behind pollution in Sungai Rambai near Taman Mutiara here. Seberang Prai City Council mayor Datuk Baderul Amin Abdul Hamid said the river is currently under the maintenance and cleaning jurisdiction of the Drainage and Irrigation Department (DID). He said the waste found at the site consisted mainly of domestic rubbish, including plastic items, food containers, beverage bottles and household waste.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM MAY 20, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed lower today, extending losses for a sixth straight session, its longest losing streak this year, as weak global sentiment weighed on investor confidence. Selling pressure hit technology, financial and export-related stocks as investors turned more defensive amid rising external uncertainties. That wraps up today's Market Pulse.

items.title: Genting Plantation posts higher Q1 net profit of RM68.06mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Genting Plantations Bhd's net profit rose to RM68.06 million in the first quarter ended March 2026 (Q1 2026) compared with RM61.25 million in the same period last year. Revenue was also marginally higher at RM720.21 million, up from RM719.45 million previously, as revenue growth across all segments was largely moderated by higher inter-segment sales. The group achieved lower crude palm oil and palm kernel prices of RM3,617 per tonne and RM3,158 per tonne, respectively.

items.title: Transport Ministry: AirBorneo may apply to operate three Sarawak intercity routes commercially
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 20 — AirBorneo may submit applications to provide flight services for the Kuching-Sibu, Kuching-Miri and Kuching-Bintulu routes on a commercial basis as the routes are not categorised as Rural Air Services (RAS). “Therefore, flights on these routes should not receive subsidies as they involve two cities, similar to any inter-city flight in Peninsular Malaysia. “The change in status of these routes to commercial routes will further encourage the participation of local airlines to operate and subsequently provide air travellers in rural Sabah and Sarawak with more flight options,” the ministry said in a statement today.

items.title: Selangor Raja Muda confers state medals on 225 recipients in conjunction with Sultan’s 80th birthday
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 20 — The Raja Muda of Selangor, Tengku Amir Shah, today conferred Selangor state medals on 225 recipients in conjunction with the 80th birthday celebration of the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah. During the investiture ceremony at the Dewan Jubli Perak Sultan Abdul Aziz here, a total of 43 individuals received the Pingat Pekerti Terpilih (P.P.T), while 35 recipients were awarded the Pingat Perkhidmatan Cemerlang Selangor (P.P.C.). ), while 13 recipients received the Pingat Perkhidmatan Selangor (P.P.S.).

items.title: Mr DIY Q1 net profit rises to RM192.02mil on higher revenue, better margins
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Mr DIY Group (M) Bhd's net profit rose to RM192.02 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 2026), from RM174.14 million in the same period last year, driven by revenue growth and improved gross margins. In a Bursa Malaysia filing, the home improvement retailer said its revenue also increased to RM1.37 billion in Q1 2026 from RM1.25 billion in the same period last year. On prospects, the group said it remains focused on delivering sustainable value through disciplined capital allocation, operational efficiency and consistent shareholder returns.

items.title: Online safety: MCMC engages public groups on age checks for under-16s
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) today held an engagement session with civil society organisations (CSOs) to discuss the implementation of online safety measures. The commission said the session also covered proposed age verification requirements for users under 16, age verification mechanisms, and the development of the Child Protection Code. Twelve CSO representatives took part in the session out of 57 organisations invited, as part of MCMC's open and inclusive approach to gathering feedback from stakeholders.

items.title: RoS cracks down on unregistered group, eight detained
items.summary: PRAI: The Registrar of Societies (RoS) detained eight people, including seven Indonesians and a local woman, during an enforcement operation against an unregistered organisation in Taman Pelangi here last night. The operation was led by Penang RoS enforcement officer Inspector Mohd Taufeeq Azmy with the assistance of the Home Ministry's Enforcement and Control Division and the police. RoS said those detained comprised four Indonesian men, three Indonesian women and a local woman believed to be holding positions within the organisation.

items.title: Sabah chief minister says rural water projects to benefit from RM4b federal allocation
items.summary: TONGOD, May 20 — Over RM4 billion in allocations has been channeled by the Federal Government to Sabah to implement Rural Water Supply (BALB) projects, which will help address the water issues faced by rural residents. Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said that the authority to implement these projects has also been devolved to the state government, and this year, over RM4 billion was channelled by the Ministry of Rural and Regional Development (KKDW) for this purpose. “Last April, the Federal Government through KKDW handed over the authority to the state government to implement Water Supply projects with an allocation of over RM4 billion.

items.title: Zara inquest: Teenager told witness she was uncomfortable with a student at times
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The late Zara Qairina Mahathir once shared feeling uncomfortable with a student who allegedly disturbed her at times but never showed signs of serious emotional distress, the Coroner's Court heard. However, the witness, who is Zara Qairina's relative, said that Zara Qairina had mentioned feeling uncomfortable with a tomboy student, although she did not appear afraid or emotionally affected by the matter. "Zara was not afraid of the student.

items.title: ITMax expands Johor footprint with RM79.9mil 'smart' order from Iskandar Puteri authority
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: ITMax System Bhd has secured a RM79.9 million variation order through its subsidiary Southmax Sdn Bhd from the Iskandar Puteri City Council (MBIP), expanding its smart infrastructure footprint in Johor. ITMax said the contract covers the deployment of additional smart traffic light controllers across Iskandar Puteri, building on the existing smart traffic system under MBIP. He added that beyond conventional traffic lights, smart traffic platform enables data-driven traffic coordination and intelligent decision-making through our in-house developed traffic simulation and management capabilities.

items.title: Repatriation of elephants from Japan requires clear evidence of welfare breaches, says Taiping council
items.summary: IPOH, May 20 — The Taiping Municipal Council (MPT) has stressed that any allegations relating to the neglect of elephant welfare at Tennoji Zoo in Japan must be supported by reasonable evidence before any move to terminate the cooperation programme or initiate the repatriation process can be considered. MPT president Mohamed Akmal Dahalan said decisions concerning the termination of the programme could not be made unilaterally as they are subject to the terms and provisions agreed upon in the agreement between the parties involved. Mohamed Akmal said the zoo is responsible for ensuring that the elephants’ welfare, health, nutrition, veterinary treatment, safety and daily management are carried out in accordance with international standards and best practices.

items.title: Ekuinas laces up retail push through XES Shoes investment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Ekuiti Nasional Bhd (Ekuinas) has acquired a controlling stake in Shellys Marketing Sdn Bhd, the operator of XES Shoes. Ekuinas chief executive officer Aliff Omar Mohamad Omar said the investment reflects the company's continued focus on supporting and advancing homegrown companies with strong growth potential. Aliff Omar said the company will work closely with the management team to strengthen its operating model, expand its retail and online presence, and deepen its reach within the mass market.

items.title: Famous Thai faith healer with rape and child sexual abuse
items.summary: BANGKOK: One of Thailand's best-known faith healers has been arrested on rape and child sexual abuse charges, police said Wednesday, as investigators urged other potential victims to come forward. Police said Wednesday they arrested self-professed healer Paisal Sanchai, 67, this month in the northern province of Lamphun and charged him with rape and sexual abuse of a minor. One of them was under 18, police said.

items.title: Kerjaya Prospek bags two contracts worth RM102mil from E&O unit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kerjaya Prospek Group Bhd's unit Kerjaya Prospek (M) Sdn Bhd has secured two new contracts for projects on Andaman Island, Penang totalling RM102.6 million. The first contract, valued at RM33.0 million, involves piling and earthworks works for a development project. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: MBSB Investment revises Malaysia's 2026 export growth forecast to 8.8pct
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MBSB Investment Bank Bhd has revised its export growth forecast for Malaysia upward to 8.8 per cent for this year as compared with 4.5 per cent last year, while import growth is projected at 7.5 per cent for the year as compared with the earlier projection of 5.0 per cent. Looking ahead, the sustained global technology upcycles and expanding artificial intelligence infrastructure will continue to drive strong demand for electrical and electronics (E&E) products. Earlier, the Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry reported that Malaysia's trade rose 28.6 per cent year-on-year to a record RM336.73 billion in April 2026, driven mainly by strong exports of E&E products.

items.title: Google and Blackstone to launch AI cloud venture
items.summary: SAN FRANCISCO: Alphabet's Google and Blackstone said on Monday that they will form an artificial intelligence (AI) cloud business venture aimed at capitalising on an insatiable demand for AI computing services. The United States-based venture will provide data centre capacity along with Google's custom AI chips, known as Tensor Processing Units, through a compute-as-a-service model. Blackstone has appointed Benjamin Sloss as chief executive officer of the new venture.

items.title: Man flees with gold item from jewellery shop in Penang
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS: Police said a man was seen fleeing with a gold item from a jewellery shop in Seberang Perai Utara, after footage of the incident went viral. The video showed a man entering the shop and asking to view jewellery from 2 sales assistants. Seberang Perai Utara assistant district police chief Superintendent C. Tharmalingam confirmed the incident.

items.title: Renewed unity govt backing cools snap polls talk
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Renewed pledges of support from parties within the unity government could help ease internal tensions and dampen speculation of an early general election, says geostrategist Azmi Hassan. He said Barisan Nasional's position, consistently reiterated by its chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, had been to support Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim until Parliament was dissolved. "The combined signals could help ease tensions between Pakatan Harapan and BN, while reducing speculation over an early dissolution of Parliament.

items.title: ECRL training programme in China building Malaysia’s future rail workforce, says Loke
items.summary: LIUZHOU (China), May 20 — The East Coast Rail Link (ECRL) Industrial Skills Training Programme (PLKI-ECRL), conducted at Liuzhou Railway Vocational Technical College (LRVTC), is helping Malaysia develop a skilled workforce for the rail industry. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said students under the year-long operations and maintenance (O&M) programme are being exposed to China’s advanced rail expertise and technical capabilities. Loke also encouraged Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institutions in Malaysia to collaborate with LRVTC to help produce more skilled talent for the railway sector.

items.title: MMM calls for immediate, transparent probe into alleged harassment of media practitioners at PH Convention
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Media Council (MMM) has called for a thorough, transparent and immediate investigation into the incident involving harassment and rough treatment against a group of media practitioners while covering the Pakatan Harapan (PH) Convention 2026 in Johor on Sunday. "Under Section 6 of the Code of Conduct, media practitioners and media organisations are bound by standards that prohibit harassment and intimidation," the statement said. MMM said media practitioners carrying out their duties lawfully and professionally must be accorded respect, space and safety to perform their responsibilities.

items.title: First batch of Malaysian ECRL trainees graduate in China ahead of 2027 rail launch
items.summary: LIUZHOU (China), May 20 — A total of 66 Malaysian trainees graduated from Liuzhou Railway Vocational Technical College (LRVTC) on Tuesday, as part of the Batch 1 East Coast Rail Link (ECRL) Industrial Skills Training Programme (PLKI-ECRL) for Operations and Maintenance (O&M) Training. The PLKI-ECRL programme is owned by Malaysia Rail Link Sdn Bhd (MRL) and China Communications Construction ECRL Sdn Bhd. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the graduates will be absorbed into the ECRL workforce, marking an important transition from trainees to professionals in Malaysia’s railway industry.

items.title: Corporatisation key to fixing Sabah water woes, says ex-director
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah's water delivery system could be improved through corporatisation, but only if it is accompanied by new expertise and structural reforms, says former Sabah Water Department director Datuk Dr Amarjit Singh. He said Sabah remained the only state in Malaysia where water services were still fully government-run, while most states in Peninsular Malaysia had successfully corporatised their water utilities. The engineer and International Water Association member said Sabah's persistent water woes might stem less from rising demand and more from weaknesses within the distribution network.

items.title: Pahang ruler's intervention ends Jerantut family's 13-year wait for electricity
items.summary: JERANTUT: A man allegedly living without electricity for more than a decade stopped the Sultan of Pahang's motorcade at Kampung Kuala Sat, Ulu Tembeling on May 17 with a placard to plead his case personally. Images of the placard bearing the words 'Ampun Tuanku, Mohon Berhenti Sekejap' (Your Highness, please stop for a moment) painted in black caught the attention of Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, who was driving a four-wheel-drive vehicle. Upon noticing the convoy approaching along the narrow stretch of road, believed to be near his house, the man quickly grabbed the placard.

items.title: Police recover ‘Rambo’ knife allegedly used in Alor Setar stabbing case
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, May 20 — Police have recovered a survival-style combat knife believed to have been used by a 32-year-old suspect in the stabbing of a woman in the neck inside a car in front of a restaurant along Jalan Titi Gajah near here on Sunday, in a case linked to jealousy and unrequited love. Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the sharp weapon, commonly referred to locally as a “Rambo knife”, was found last Monday about 200 metres from the scene in bushes near a paddy field area. Meanwhile, Adzli said the police would dispose of prohibited substances at the Bukit Pelanduk Solid Waste Disposal Centre in Negeri Sembilan, involving various types of drugs and prohibited substances worth an estimated RM12.39 million that had completed legal proceedings and received court disposal orders.

items.title: Sarawak plans own space agency and aims to launch first nanosatellite by 2030, says Abang Johari
items.summary: KUCHING, May 20 — Sarawak is planning to establish its own space agency, said Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Openg. “Apart from that, the Sarawak Aerospace Advisory Council (SAAC) has been established to provide strategic direction and comprehensive guidance in driving the establishment of Sarawak’s aerospace sector, in line with current global industry standards,” he said when winding up the debate on the Sarawak Premier’s Department on the motion of thanks to the Yang Dipertua Negeri at the Sarawak State Legislative Assembly here today. Abang Johari said Sarawak is also targeting the launch of Sarawak’s first nanosatellite by 2030, through CubeSat-based technology which involves the use of small satellites for real-time data and earth observation.

items.title: ‘Anything is possible’: Negeri Sembilan MB does not rule out state election this year
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 20 — Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun has not ruled out the possibility of the state election being held this year, although the matter is still under consideration. The Negeri Sembilan Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman said the possibility of holding the election simultaneously with neighbouring states Melaka and Johor would also be looked into. “The possibility is there (for a state election).

items.title: Bursa Malaysia closes lower amid weak global risk sentiment
items.summary: Among heavyweights, Maybank and Public Bank fell two sen each to RM11.06 and RM4.77 respectively, CIMB shed eight sen to RM7.76, Tenaga Nasional slipped six sen to RM14.46, and IHH Healthcare eased one sen to RM8.99. Capital A eased one sen to 43 sen, while Hong Seng Consolidated was flat at one sen. As for top losers, Petronas Gas fell 52 sen to RM17.20, Petronas Dagangan shed 38 sen to RM18.82, Wasco declined 20.5 sen to 89.5 sen, while VSTECS and PPB Group slipped 18 sen each to RM5.75 and RM10.30, respectively.

items.title: Federal Court cuts ex-university student’s cannabis sentence from 30 years to 12
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 20 — The Federal Court today reduced the prison sentence of a former private university student from 30 years to 12 years and 10 strokes of the cane after he was found in possession of 1,900.9 grammes of cannabis six years ago. A three-judge panel chaired by Justice Rhodzariah Bujang unanimously allowed the final appeal by Muhammad Nur Arif Nur Akil, 28, to set aside the Court of Appeal’s conviction and sentence delivered on May 26, 2025, and substitute it with a conviction for possession. However, the Court of Appeal on May 26, 2025, upheld the High Court’s decision after dismissing his appeal.

items.title: [UPDATED] Police question MalaysiaNow editor over defamation probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MalaysiaNow editor-in-chief Abd Rahman Koya was called in today to assist investigations linked to an article involving a video clip allegedly connected to businessman Datuk Seri Farhash Wafa Salvador Rizal Mubarak, police said. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said Abd Rahman's statement was recorded under Section 500 of the Penal Code for defamation. Abd Rahman earlier arrived at the Petaling Jaya district police headquarters about 2.15pm, accompanied by lawyer N. Rajesh.

items.title: Han Keong's dream to continue studies at polytechnic cut short
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: A young man's dream of pursuing automotive studies at a polytechnic was cut short after he was killed in a horrific crash at Jalan Losong Feri yesterday. Eighteen-year-old Wah Han Keong, the elder of two siblings, was pronounced dead at the scene from severe neck injuries. Tee Tun said his son had been passionate about automotive studies and had already applied through the Central University Admissions Unit (UPU) to further his education at either a polytechnic in Dungun or Kota Baru.

items.title: Bintulu Port transfer to be finalised by end June, says Uggah
items.summary: KUCHING, May 20 — The handing over of Bintulu Port to Sarawak will be finalised by the end of June this year, said Sarawak Deputy Premier Datuk Amar Douglas Uggah Embas. He said the federal government has agreed to the transfer of Bintulu Port to Sarawak government for a value of RM1.8 billion. In other developments, Uggah who is also Sarawak infrastructure and port development minister, said as of April this year, Sarawak-Sabah Link Road (SSLR) Phase 1 has achieved 70.05 percent physical progress and is expected to be completed in 2027.

items.title: Five men arrested after viral cable theft video in Pasir Gudang
items.summary: SERI ALAM: Police arrested five men suspected of being involved in cable theft in Pasir Gudang after a clip of the incident went viral on social media. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Sohaimi Ishak said police detected a seven-second clip uploaded on Threads by the account "@inhuman.13", which showed several men using two vehicles to remove cables. He said preliminary investigations showed the theft occurred at 4.15am on May 18.

items.title: Federal Court maintains 30-year sentence, 16 strokes for Ukrainian over 24kg meth haul
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 20 — The Federal Court here today upheld the 30-year prison sentence and 16 strokes of the cane imposed on a Ukrainian national for trafficking 24.753 kg of methamphetamine four years ago. A three-member bench comprising Justices Datuk Rhodzariah Bujang, Datuk Lee Swee Seng and Datuk Ravinthran N. Paramaguru dismissed the appeal filed by Akahimo (also known as Akanimo) against his conviction and sentence. On June 26, 2024, the High Court found Akahimo guilty and sentenced him to 30 years in prison with 16 strokes of the cane.

items.title: [UPDATED] Police investigate Tony Pua over FB post on royal decrees, Rukun Negara
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have launched an investigation against former Damansara member of parliament Tony Pua over a Facebook post commenting on issues surrounding royal decrees and the Rukun Negara. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk Seri M. Kumar said the investigation was initiated after police received 28 reports nationwide over the posting. When asked whether police would call Pua in to assist in the investigation, Kumar said this would be done in the near future.

items.title: Berjaya Air debuts world-first ATR business cabin [WATCH]
items.summary: SUBANG: Berjaya Air has launched the world's first ATR 72-600 aircraft featuring an all-Business Class HighLine cabin configuration as the airline positions itself in the premium regional and charter travel segment. The Malaysian carrier took delivery of the aircraft here on Wednesday, marking the debut of ATR's new HighLine all-Business Class cabin concept globally. Voo said the ATR 72-600 HighLine aircraft features only 26 seats in an all-Business Class layout aimed at offering an exclusive and premium travel experience.

items.title: RMAF confirms F/A-18D Hornet lands safely at KKIA after engine trouble mid-flight
items.summary: LABUAN, May 20 — The Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) has confirmed that an F/A-18D Hornet aircraft, deployed on a detachment at the Labuan Air Base for training purposes, experienced an engine issue during its return flight after undergoing a night Air-to-Surface Firing Exercise in Kota Belud, Sabah, yesterday. “The aircraft landed safely at 7:47pm,” the statement read. Following the incident, the RMAF deployed a technical team to KKIA to conduct inspections and provide technical assistance for the aircraft.

items.title: Sabah police busted two trafficking syndicates, seize drugs worth over RM3.25mil
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah police have crippled two drug trafficking syndicates, including one distributing Tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) laced vape products, with seizures worth more than RM3.25 million in raids across four districts recently. Sabah Police Commissioner Datuk Jauteh Dikun said the seizures included more than 63kg of syabu, 31 kg of THC liquid, 4.49g of cannabis and 1kg of ecstasy powder. He said the seizure involved 10,000 vape cartridges weighing an estimated 20kg together with two blue gallon bottles containing liquid suspected to be THC weighing 11kg, with the total seizure valued at about RM93,000.

items.title: Johor exco vows action after MBJB contractors caught on CCTV running over dog
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 19 — The Johor Bahru City Council (MBJB) will take action after identifying contractors involved in deliberately running over and killing a dog in a pick-up truck here last week. The incident, which was captured on closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage, has since triggered public outrage against a team of MBJB contractors during a stray dog eradication operation in Puteri Wangsa here on May 15. Johor Housing and Local Government Committee chairman Datuk Mohd Jafni Md Shukor said MBJB’s internal investigation had identified the individuals involved as appointed contractors carrying out the stray dog eradication operation.

items.title: Show-cause letter issued after alleged stray dog incident in Puteri Wangsa
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A show-cause letter has been issued to a contractor and a police report lodged after CCTV footage allegedly showed a stray dog being run over in Puteri Wangsa, with authorities vowing firm action, including possible termination and legal steps. Johor Housing and Local Government Committee chairman Mohd Jafni Md Shukor said the Johor Baru City Council (MBJB) would not tolerate breaches of procedures in enforcement operations involving stray animals. He said enforcement work must be carried out in a controlled and humane manner, in full compliance with procedures set by the Housing and Local Government Ministry.

items.title: DBKL offers business licence renewals of up to 3 years
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Kuala Lumpur City Hall's (DBKL) initiative to introduce business licence renewals of up to three years beginning July 1 is expected to benefit nearly 95,000 licence holders while strengthening a more business-friendly and efficient urban business ecosystem. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said the initiative was introduced to ease the burden of annual licence renewals, which often place pressure on traders, especially towards the end of the year. Yeoh said DBKL would offer a five per cent discount on licence fees for two-year renewals and 10 per cent for three-year renewals.

items.title: Man pleads not guilty in Kedah to using other individuals’ MyKad numbers to buy subsidised petrol
items.summary: BALING, May 20 — A man pleaded not guilty at the Magistrate’s Court here today to 13 charges of using other people’s identity card numbers to purchase Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) petrol in this district. Azman Aziz, 53, was accused of impersonating others by using the identity card numbers of three individuals to purchase 594.459 litres of subsidised petrol under the Budi Madani scheme, causing the complainants to lose their monthly subsidised fuel quotas. Sessions Court Judge Rohaida Ishak, acting as Magistrate, allowed the accused bail of RM15,000 and set July 8 for case mention.

items.title: Sabah Customs foils drug smuggling attempt worth over RM3.1m involving courier parcels and airport luggage
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, May 20 — The Royal Malaysian Customs Department of Sabah successfully foiled attempts to distribute various types of drugs worth over RM3,153,650 through three separate seizures at the Tawau International Airport and the Pos Aviation Warehouse in Kota Kinabalu recently. Sabah State Customs Director, Dr Ahmad Taufik Sulaiman, said that at around 1.35pm on May 11, officers on duty inspected luggage at the Tawau Airport after scanning images detected suspicious contents inside four bags at the Pos Aviation Warehouse. “In the second case, the Sabah JKDM Narcotics Branch seized a package suspected to contain 4,678 grammes of methamphetamine worth RM233,900 at the Pos Aviation Warehouse in Kota Kinabalu on May 11.

items.title: Simen Rahmah scheme continues with 1.6 million tonnes cement allocation for affordable housing, says minister
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 20 — The Simen Rahmah Scheme will continue this year with 1.6 million metric tonnes of cement allocated to support the target of building 500,000 affordable housing units under the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP). Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said the scheme, which offers cement at prices lower than the market rate, has also been expanded to cover affordable housing projects under the Federal Government, state governments and the private sector. “The first phase of the Simen Rahmah initiative will provide 1.6 million metric tonnes of cement to support affordable housing projects priced at RM300,000 and below per unit.

items.title: Fahmi: MyKad-based age verification already standard practice in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the use of official documents such as MyKad for age verification is already a common practice in Malaysia and is not inconsistent with existing digital verification systems. He said identity verification using official documents is widely adopted across various sectors, particularly in e-wallet services and other digital platforms. He also said the Personal Data Protection Act would apply to the handling and management of personal data throughout such verification processes.

items.title: Two police reports lodged over claims on Taman Segar housing status change, says Hannah Yeoh
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 20 — Two police reports have been lodged against allegations that houses in Taman Segar, Cheras had been changed from leasehold to freehold, Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said. She described the allegations, which also claimed that an announcement was made about the matter at a media conference in Taman Segar regarding the gazettement of a field for public use, as slander because such an announcement was never made. “Police reports have been lodged, two police reports.

items.title: DBKL to offer up to three-year business licence renewals from July 1, says FT minister
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 20 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall’s (DBKL) initiative to introduce business licence renewals of up to three years beginning July 1 is expected to benefit nearly 95,000 licence holders while strengthening a more business-friendly and efficient urban business ecosystem. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said the initiative was introduced to ease the burden of annual licence renewals, which often place pressure on traders, especially towards the end of the year. Hannah said DBKL would offer a five per cent discount on licence fees for two-year renewals and 10 per cent for three-year renewals.

items.title: Sarawak projects RM13.04b revenue in 2026, says deputy premier
items.summary: KUCHING, May 20 — Sarawak is projecting a revenue of RM13.04 billion to be collected this year, said Sarawak Deputy Premier and Second Minister for Finance and New Economy Datuk Amar Douglas Uggah Embas. Uggah said that as of April, RM3.86 billion, or 29.6 per cent of the projected amount, has been collected. Uggah said given the prevailing economic headwinds and geopolitical uncertainties affecting global oil and gas markets, the state government foresee an impact on this year’s revenue projection.

items.title: New trains coming in 2031: Rapid Rail plans 26-unit Kelana Jaya LRT fleet upgrade to boost capacity and reduce disruptions
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 20 — A total of 26 new trains for the Kelana Jaya Light Rail Transit (LRT) Line are expected to begin operating in 2031 in an effort to increase capacity and operational efficiency as well as reduce service disruptions to users. LRT Rapid Rail chief operating officer, Nor Azmi Mohd Yusof said the process of replacing existing trains had begun with the first train scheduled to arrive in 2029 before the entire replacement is targeted to be completed by the end of 2030. Commenting on the increase in the number of users, Nor Azmi said the load factor for the Kelana Jaya Line had reached 109 per cent in April.

items.title: Female teacher in Seremban faces five sexual assault charges involving 14-year-old male student
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 20 — A female teacher pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court today to five counts of physical and non-physical sexual assault against a 14-year-old male student, dating back to September last year. For two counts of physical sexual assault, the class teacher allegedly committed the act under a tree outside an apartment here between Sept 12 and 20, 2025, around 2.30 pm. The accused faces an additional physical sexual assault charge under Section 377B of the Penal Code, which carries a similar penalty.

items.title: Survey shows Malaysians becoming more intolerant of differences amid rising ‘insular trust’ trend
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — More Malaysians are becoming less tolerant of people with differing opinions and values, according to the 2026 Edelman Trust Barometer, which points to a deepening “insular trust” mindset and widening social polarisation. Despite improving overall trust in institutions such as businesses and the government, the report suggests a worrying trend: Malaysians are increasingly retreating into familiar information environments and are less willing to trust those with different values, political views or cultural backgrounds. On average, 65 per cent of Malaysians now exhibit an insular trust mindset, while only 37 per cent regularly seek information from sources with differing political viewpoints, the survey found.

items.title: Pakatan secretariat to review media complaint over alleged rough treatment at convention, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said he has instructed the Pakatan Harapan (PH) Convention secretariat to review a complaint lodged by the Johor Media Club (KMJ) over an alleged rough incident involving media practitioners during the convention on Sunday. “As the director of the convention, I deeply regret the incident. On Tuesday, KMJ lodged a police report over alleged aggressive behaviour by security personnel towards media practitioners covering the 2026 PH Convention in Johor Bahru on Sunday.

items.title: Fahmi says Putrajaya’s expenditure realignment exercise reaches 50pc of target
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today said the government’s expenditure realignment exercise has achieved about 50 per cent of its target so far. He said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim informed the Cabinet that measures implemented across ministries based on current priorities had begun to show positive results. “The prime minister also informed the Cabinet that expenditure realignment measures implemented across ministries based on current priorities had begun to show positive results without affecting service delivery or the implementation of ministry programmes.

items.title: BN, GRS and GPS reaffirm support for unity government until end of term, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had informed the Cabinet that Barisan Nasional (BN), Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) and Gabungan Parti Sarawak (GPS) had reaffirmed their support for the unity government and pledged to remain with the administration until the end of the current term. Fahmi said the prime minister also expressed appreciation during today’s Cabinet meeting for the continued commitment shown by component parties in the Madani government. “He said the parties had reaffirmed their support for the unity government and their commitment to remain with the administration until the end of the current term.

items.title: Sultan Ibrahim confers state honours to Johor Regent’s wife and Raja Muda
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 20 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today conferred the ‘Darjah Sultan Ibrahim Johor Yang Amat Disanjungi Pangkat Pertama Dato’ Sri Mulia Sultan Ibrahim Johor (SMIJ)’ state title upon Che’ Puan Mahkota Khaleeda Johor, wife of Johor Regent Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim. According to a post on the official Facebook page of HRH Crown Prince of Johor, His Majesty also conferred the ‘Darjah Kerabat Johor Yang Amat Dihormati Pangkat Pertama (DK I)’ upon the Raja Muda of Johor, Tunku Iskandar Tunku Ismail. Also in attendance were Tunku Temenggong of Johor, Tunku Idris Iskandar Al-Haj Sultan Ibrahim, and Tunku Abu Bakar Ibrahim Tunku Ismail.

items.title: Brother among five charged over teen’s murder after alleged cable theft dispute
items.summary: IPOH, May 20 — A man and four of his friends were charged with murdering his younger brother in Tanjung Rambutan last week, at the Magistrate’s Court here today. Siri Sakti Vell, 22, and his friends K. Thirucelvan, 33, Shashipal Singh Manmuhan Singh, 36, N. Thaneshwaran, 24, and P. Ukenthiran, 35, together with one other individual who remains at large, are accused of murdering S.K. They allegedly committed the act in the vicinity of the Sri Subramaniar Temple, Jalan Perpaduan Southward, Tanjung Rambutan, between 1 am and 4 am on May 12.
